title: Avoid modifying collections during iteration
description: Modifying a collection while iterating over it can cause runtime exceptions and unpredictable behavior. This rule ensures that collections are not changed during iteration, which helps maintain program stability and prevents subtle bugs.
recommendation: Do not add or remove elements from a collection while iterating over it. Use iterator methods or collect changes to apply after iteration.

noncompliant: for (String item : list) { if (item.isEmpty()) list.remove(item); } // Causes ConcurrentModificationException
compliant: Iterator<String> it = list.iterator(); while (it.hasNext()) { if (it.next().isEmpty()) it.remove(); }

checks.message: Do not add or remove elements from a collection while iterating over it. Use iterator methods or collect changes to apply after iteration.
